title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the ACTIVE engineering backlog (open + guardrail items; replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). Completed (Done) items are archived in data/backlog_archive.json to keep this file lean — the admin Backlog panel reads BOTH and merges them, so done items stay visible. The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay in the JSON.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item. ARCHIVE (post-split): completed items now live in `data/backlog_archive.json`. When you set an item to `done` and strip its prompt, MOVE the item object out of backlog.json into backlog_archive.json (the admin panel merges both). Recurring items never move — they stay open in backlog.json.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`app/store.js`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/render code.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt: RECURRING — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Each run is a fresh full audit (this is the Nth, not a one-shot); the findings ship as NEW backlog items, and R1 itself stays open for the next pass. Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo — architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for the CURRENT architecture (ADR-001) AFTER the Svelte/TypeScript reorg. Specifically: a Vite multi-page build (vite.config.mjs + the vite-ssg.mjs plugin) to `dist/` (A26), served by Cloudflare Pages + `/functions/*` edge functions (TypeScript, A78). The source tree is `src/{lib,app,site}` (A30): src/lib/ is the framework-agnostic pure-logic core in native TS (A61) — core.ts (compute/cost/format/event bus/pure helpers), adapters.ts, store.ts, demostore.ts, curveseries.ts, report.ts, sampledata.ts, format.ts, types.ts — reused verbatim per A29; src/app/ is the Svelte 5 SPA on ALL app surfaces after the A33 cutover (app/demo/staging are marker-free mount points loading src/app/main.ts -> App.svelte; components in src/app/components/; app-only glue in src/app/lib/; the vanilla view layer was deleted); src/site/ is the marketing/info Svelte SSG (A69 — components prerendered by vite-ssg.mjs, hydrated in place). Constraints to verify against: runes-only Svelte 5 (no `export let`/`$:`/createEventDispatcher/svelte-store writables), `src/` is `any`-free TS, the mode-aware Store seam via context('bb:store') (real IndexedDB for app/staging, in-memory DemoStore for demo — demo NEVER persists or mutates), `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55 — no inline styles), design tokens only in src/styles/tokens.css, .ts is linted (A79) and typecheck = tsc(core) + tsc(functions) + svelte-check(app), and two-track automated versioning (CH12, scripts/bump-version.mjs). THE hard product constraint: compute is local — no trade data ever leaves the browser. Give concrete, prioritized findings (P1/P2/P3 with file:line), and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments scaffold: never provision an account/entitlement in `functions/api/webhook.js` before verifying the Stripe signature — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, constant-time compare, and a timestamp/replay window. The endpoint is an inert 501 stub today; reject any future change that wires up provisioning without signature verification first.

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Since the A33 cutover all three surfaces (app/demo/staging) are ONE Svelte SPA under src/app/ — there is no separate staging codebase to copy. Staging-only behavior lives behind `STAGING_PAGE` runtime guards INSIDE the shared components; promotion = removing those guards so the feature ships on every surface.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request; if none are named, promote the full staging delta (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Inventory with `grep -rn STAGING_PAGE src/` — each hit is a feature gate. The staging ENVIRONMENT infra (isolated IndexedDB, the sample seed, the LS_*_staging layout namespacing, the key-gate in functions/_middleware.ts) keys off `PAGE_MODE === 'staging'`, NOT STAGING_PAGE, so it stays put. List each feature + its gate(s) before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) flip the gate to unconditional — e.g. `STAGING_PAGE ? a : b` -> `a`, `{#if STAGING_PAGE}` -> unconditional, `class:staging={STAGING_PAGE}` + `.overlay.staging` CSS -> `.overlay`; remove the now-unused STAGING_PAGE import. (2) DEMO 1:1 — the feature must work on demo, but keep demo NON-MUTATING: any TRADE-writing control stays `disabled` under `isDemo` and guarded (`if (isDemo) return;`), and the in-memory DemoStore means UI/layout prefs (saved layouts, hidden modules) simply don't persist — that's correct, not a bug. (3) keep it CSP-clean (no inline style="" — A55) and a11y-intact.

THEN: `npm test` (lint+typecheck+format+unit) + `npm run test:e2e` (boots all surfaces). UPDATE e2e: any spec asserting demo/prod LACKS the feature (e.g. 'demo … no menu', 'demo … not paginated') must flip to assert it now HAS it. Verify in a browser (build -> serve dist/ -> Playwright) that the demo + app surfaces show the feature. Title the PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level — do NOT hand-edit data/versions.json (staging keeps its lead). Add a data/changelog.json (F13) entry at the top of `releases`, keyed to the NEW prod version, describing what shipped for users. The bump-version STAGING_ONLY set is just `src/app/staging.html` (the env page) — features were never in it, so no change there.

Each run done when: the in-scope features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo non-mutating), all surfaces verified, npm test + e2e green, a changelog entry added, committed/pushed (PR only if asked). Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

items.title: Discussion & planning: Trade Replay (simulated market snapshots)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements for a Trade Replay feature that lets users step through simulated market snapshots in time around a trade to review it for analysis. Cover: what data is needed (do current platform exports carry intra-trade price/time, or only fills? what extra market data would replay require and from where?), storage/perf implications for a client-side static-hosted app, the UI surface, and a phased build estimate. Done when: a written feasibility/effort estimate exists with concrete next-step items and open questions.

items.title: Discussion & planning: economic calendar data on the calendar module
items.prompt: Scope adding economic-event data (FOMC and other standard futures-relevant events) to the calendar module, user-togglable, with ~5 years of historical events backed in. Assess: data sourcing/licensing, how/where the dataset is stored and shipped (static JSON? payload size for 5 years?), how events render on the calendar without crowding the existing trade/journal data, the toggle UX, and ongoing updates/maintenance. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Stripe integration (donations now, subscriptions later)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements to set up Stripe. NEAR-TERM goal: accept donations — the user clicks/interacts with the pricing module on the homepage to trigger a Stripe donation flow. Cover: Stripe primitives for one-off donations (Payment Links vs Checkout Session vs Payment Element), what server side is needed on Cloudflare Pages (a Pages Function for the Checkout session + webhook, secret/key handling — no keys in the static client), the homepage UX hook on the existing pricing element, and test vs live mode. ALSO lay down scaffolding for monthly SUBSCRIPTIONS (Products/Prices, customer + billing portal, webhooks) even though subscriptions are a later goal gated on remote data storage + user accounts — note the dependency and what can be stubbed now vs deferred. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements (donations MVP path, subscription scaffolding, server/secret needs, open questions) + concrete next-step backlog items exist.

items.title: Add a copyright + operator line to the site footer
items.prompt: From R7. The shared site footer (partials/footer.html) has no copyright line and never names the operating entity. Add a '© 2026 <name>' line (and, on legal.html, a 'Blotterbook is operated by <entity>' note). The exact entity display name is a pending owner decision (TBD as of R7 — 'Scaline Audio' per LICENSE); ship brand-only '© 2026 Blotterbook' now if undecided and swap in the entity once chosen. Rebuild includes so every page picks up the footer change. Done when: every page footer shows a copyright line and the operator is named once the entity is decided.

items.title: Pre-monetization legal pack: ToS, Privacy Policy, Donations/Refund policy
items.prompt: From R7 — prerequisite for R15 (Stripe). Before taking money, expand the legal content (legal.html / new sections or pages): (1) a real Terms of Service distinct from the current disclaimer bullets, with a governing-law/venue clause set to DELAWARE; (2) a standalone full Privacy Policy (today it's a summary) covering local-only storage, the coarse-region tax lookup, and any Stripe data once payments land; (3) a Donations/Refund policy stating the $25/$50 tiers are voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations with no goods/services exchanged; (4) name the operating entity / seller identity (BLOCKED on the owner's entity decision from R7). Keep the existing not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers. Link the new docs from the footer and from the donation/checkout flow. Done when: ToS (Delaware), Privacy Policy, and the non-refundable Donations/Refund policy are published and linked, and the seller identity is shown.

items.title: (staging) Review: purpose of the 'Tagged trades' Overview stat + what tagging means
items.prompt: Staging-scoped discussion. Review the 'Tagged trades' stat in the Manage data → Overview section: where its value comes from today (the count of trades in the trademeta store carrying tags), what tagging a trade currently MEANS in the app (per-trade tags set in the trade editor, surfaced in the dashboard Tag filter), and what it SHOULD mean as a workflow (setups? mistakes? strategies? review state?). Decide whether the Overview stat earns its place, how it should be defined/labeled, and whether tagging needs a clearer purpose or UX. Any resulting UI change is staging-only (proving-ground) until promoted via CH16. Done when: a written decision on the stat's meaning + tagging's intended purpose exists, with concrete next-step items if changes are warranted.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Rate-data scraper must stay within the 50-subrequest free-tier cap
items.prompt: Guardrail for the roadmap's scheduled rate-data scraper (the Cron Worker that will pull CME fee schedules, per-broker data-feed prices, and per-state tax tables into `data/*.json`). The Workers FREE plan allows only 50 EXTERNAL subrequests per invocation. When this is built, design within that cap: batch/limit outbound fetches to <=50 per run (count the GitHub commit-back call), split sources across multiple scheduled invocations, or move to Workers Paid. Reject a single-invocation design that fans out to dozens of sources on the free tier.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Move to Workers Paid before shipping the paid cloud-sync tier
items.prompt: Guardrail for the planned cloud-sync subscription tier (the `CloudStore` behind the `Store` seam, A4). Every device push/pull is a Pages Function invocation against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day cap — a few hundred active sync users would exhaust it — and KV's 1,000 writes/day free cap is far too low for user data. Do NOT ship a paying sync product on the free Workers plan: treat the Workers Paid plan ($5/mo — 10M requests + higher KV/CPU) as a prerequisite, with R2 (10 GB free, free egress — fits the zero-knowledge model) for encrypted trade blobs and D1/KV for accounts/entitlements. Plan the economics around Workers Paid (trivial against subscription revenue), not around stretching the free tier. Reject a sync rollout that assumes the free request/KV caps.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Keep Workers KV low-write — never store per-visitor state in KV
items.prompt: Guardrail for KV usage (`STATUS_KV`). The Workers FREE plan caps KV at 1,000 WRITES/day (plus 1,000 deletes + 1,000 lists; reads are 100k/day; 1 GB storage). Today KV holds only admin-set status + feature-flag config and the best-effort admin rate-limiter — all low-write and fail-open, which is fine. Keep it that way: never put per-VISITOR or per-REQUEST state in KV (counters, analytics, sessions, public-endpoint rate-limiting) — that would blow 1,000 writes/day almost immediately and start erroring. New per-user/per-request persistence belongs in D1/R2 (and on Workers Paid). Reject a change that writes to KV on a public or high-traffic path.

items.title: Guardrail: admin-endpoint security must not depend on the fail-open rate limiter
items.prompt: From the R1 audit. rateLimited() (functions/_lib/http.js ~19-30) returns false (ALLOW) whenever STATUS_KV is unbound and is best-effort/non-atomic; it is the only throttle on /api/status, /api/config, and /api/admin-key (token issuance, 20/60s). This is the correct trade-off (don't lock the admin out; endpoints sit behind Access + constant-time token/key compare), so it is defense-in-depth only. Guardrail: never let an endpoint's security DEPEND on rateLimited() — auth (Access JWT + constant-time compare) is the real control; reject any future endpoint that relies on the limiter as primary protection, and keep STATUS_KV bound in prod so the throttle is actually active. Adjacent: A17 (KV low-write), S11 (webhook signature).

items.title: (guardrail) Dev tooling stays dev-only — SUPERSEDED IN PART by ADR-001/A28
items.prompt: PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md. This guardrail was written for the R19 Tier A world where the shipped app was zero-dep and the committed files were the deploy artifact (so devDeps could never touch shipped bytes, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 was set on Pages). ADR-001 reverses that: Tier B (Vite build) is being adopted, so build deps now DO produce shipped bytes and Pages WILL install + build. The replaced clauses: 'shipped output stays dependency-free', the drift gate as proof-of-no-shipped-deps, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL — those no longer hold after A26 lands. WHAT STILL APPLIES until/after migration: keep node_modules + build output (playwright-report, test-results, the Vite dist/output dir) gitignored; pin every dep with a committed lockfile. The NEW dependency discipline lives in A28 (minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain as a security control; local-compute pillar gates every dep). Reference A28, not this item, going forward.

items.title: (guardrail) Dependency policy — minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain is a security control
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md), replacing the zero-runtime-dep stance (A25). Runtime/build dependencies are now ALLOWED, under discipline — because for a privacy product handling financial data, the supply chain IS a security control. (1) EARN-ITS-WEIGHT: add a dep only when hand-rolling it is materially worse; keep writing small utilities by hand (assets/util.js). Heavyweight enablers qualify (the framework, charting like lightweight-charts, layout/virtualization libs); date/utility micro-libs do not. (2) SUPPLY CHAIN: pin every version + commit the lockfile; run `npm audit` in CI; apply Subresource Integrity / strict CSP to shipped bundles; keep the dependency surface minimal; consider vendoring the few critical libs so a registry compromise can't silently reach users. (3) LOCAL-COMPUTE PILLAR GATES EVERY DEP (hard): reject any dep or dep feature that phones home, ships telemetry/analytics, or could exfiltrate trade data — no exceptions, regardless of convenience. Reject any dependency addition that fails the earn-its-weight bar or the local-compute gate.

items.title: (guardrail) Preserve the pure-logic core verbatim through the framework migration
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). The Svelte migration (A27 + later Phase 4) must touch the VIEW LAYER ONLY. The pure-logic modules are the hard-won, test-covered value and must be imported into components VERBATIM, not rewritten: app/adapters.js (platform CSV adapters + detection + pairFills), compute() (metrics), costModel() (commissions/subscriptions/tax/take-home), the Section-1256 tax model, app/store.js (the Store seam — A4), and assets/util.js. They stay framework-agnostic so the node test suites (test-adapters/test-version/test-auth/test-flags/test-tax) keep covering them unchanged. Reject a framework PR that reimplements adapter parsing, the metrics/cost/tax math, or persistence inside a component instead of importing the existing module. The Store interface (A4) remains the only persistence seam. TYPESCRIPT CLARIFICATION (A61, docs/typescript-decision.md): converting the core from JSDoc-typed .js to native .ts (folded into the A30 reorg) is PERMITTED — it changes the syntax host (.js→.ts, JSDoc→inline types), NOT the algorithms. 'Verbatim' here means NO logic rewrite, not 'never touch the file'. The gate is that the node suites pass IDENTICALLY before/after the conversion (they are the behavior-preserving safety net). Still reject any change that reimplements the parsing/metrics/cost/tax/persistence logic under cover of the TS migration.

items.title: Discuss: which Svelte features to utilize
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Survey Svelte 5 features we under-use and where they'd help in the runes-first SPA: $derived/$effect patterns, snippets, stores vs. context (we use context('bb:store')), transitions/animations, actions (we have styleProps/modal), lazy/async components, error boundaries, and the compiler a11y warnings. Map each to current/roadmap UI (curve, calendar, the module menu R12, resizable modules R13, blotter F23). Benefit vs. complexity; recommendation + follow-ups. Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered.

items.title: CRITICAL: exhaustive audit of all data calculations
items.prompt: P1 - CRITICAL. Exhaustive correctness audit of EVERY calculation Blotterbook performs on imported data - this is the platform's core value and must be air-tight. Scope (pure-logic core, A29): app/adapters.js (CSV parse, fills pairing via pairFills(), normalization, symbol/root, side, qty, holdMs, date/timezone handling); app/core.js compute() (PnL, win rate, profit factor, expectancy, equity curve + drawdown, streaks, day/session bucketing, averages); app/core.js costModel() (per-broker commission tiers, exchange/clearing/NFA fees, micros, data-feed + platform subscriptions, Section-1256 tax via state-tax.json, blended rate, take-home); app/curveseries.js (gross/net/take series); app/report.js. Verify against hand-worked examples and edge cases: empty/partial fills, same-symbol reversals, multi-contract, fractional/large qty, missing fields, DST/year boundaries (note isoWeek tweak A58), float-accumulation rounding, negative/zero PnL, micros vs minis, per-state tax. Confirm the node fixtures (test-adapters/test-tax) are sufficient and add cases for gaps. READ-ONLY audit: file a fix item for each discrepancy, don't fix inline. Done when: a thorough, prioritized written audit covering every calc path is delivered with fix items filed.

items.title: Discuss/explore: deeper Svelte 5 runes + reactivity adoption
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. The app is already runes-only (no `export let` / `$:` / createEventDispatcher; state is `$state` / `$derived` / `$effect`, with cross-component state via `context('bb:store')` + component runes). Survey where it could lean further into Svelte 5's reactivity model for clarity and perf: extracting shared reactive state into `.svelte.ts` rune modules (none exist today) vs the current context approach; `$derived.by` for the heavier App.svelte computed chains; `$effect` cleanup/teardown correctness; `$bindable()` props where parent/child two-way binding is currently faked with callbacks; `$state.snapshot` at the persistence boundaries; and fine-grained vs coarse reactivity in the dashboard. Use the Svelte MCP docs (list-sections / get-documentation) as the reference. For each: benefit vs churn and fit with A28 (deps) / A29 (verbatim core). Recommendation plus any spun-off items. Adjacent: A65 (which Svelte features to utilize). Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered.

items.title: (optional) Root tidy: relocate vite-ssg.mjs + drop dead tsconfig allowJs
items.prompt: OPTIONAL low-priority tidy spun off from A82. Two independent micro-cleanups: (1) vite-ssg.mjs is the only repo-root file nothing discovers (it's a plain relative import from vite.config.mjs) — it could move into scripts/ (the deploy contract already pins scripts/ at the root) with a one-line import change; weigh the one-fewer-root-file benefit against updating the deploy-contract notes in CLAUDE.md/docs/architecture.md. (2) tsconfig.json still sets allowJs:true / checkJs:false and carries a comment about the A69 'transition' — A69 is complete and there is no hand-written .js under src/ anymore, so both options are now dead and the comment is stale; verify `npm run typecheck` (tsc + svelte-check) still passes after removing them. Neither is required; ship only if touching this area anyway. Done when: applied (or explicitly declined with a note).
items.doneNote: Both applied. (1) Moved vite-ssg.mjs → scripts/vite-ssg.mjs (git mv); the move needed 3 code edits, not the optimistically-assumed one — vite.config.mjs import path, the plugin's projectRoot (now resolve(import.meta.dirname,'..') to stay repo-root), and its svelte.config import (../). Updated every deploy-contract/doc reference: CLAUDE.md (repo-layout tree + 2 md links + 2 inline mentions) and docs/architecture.md (lockstep table + SSG md link). Build + all 28 e2e (incl. ssg.spec) green, confirming SSR still resolves. (2) Removed the dead allowJs:true/checkJs:false from tsconfig.json and rewrote the stale A69-'transition' comment; npm run typecheck (tsc core + functions + svelte-check) passes with 0 errors.

items.title: Vite/CI build hygiene: explicit build.target, size budget, de-dup CI build
items.prompt: Spun off from A64 + A67. Low-risk build/CI hygiene, all A28-clean (dev-only): (1) set an explicit Vite build.target (e.g. es2022, matching tsconfig) for deterministic output — do NOT add @vitejs/plugin-legacy (polyfills/deps against A28 for a modern-browser audience). (2) Add a dev-only bundle size-budget check (a small script asserting the /app/ entry JS stays under a byte ceiling, or rollup-plugin-visualizer as a dev devDep) so bundle regressions fail loudly. (3) De-duplicate the CI double-build: ci.yml runs `npm run build` AND test:e2e's Playwright webServer also runs `npm run build` — either drop the standalone build step (e2e proves the build) or point the e2e webServer at the already-built dist/ (serve, don't rebuild). (4) Optional: cache the Playwright browser (actions/cache keyed on the pinned @playwright/test version) and add a CI concurrency group (cancel-in-progress superseded PR runs). Done when: build.target + size budget shipped and the CI build de-dup decided/applied.
items.doneNote: All four shipped, zero new deps (A28). (1) vite.config build.target:'es2022' matching tsconfig — no plugin-legacy. (2) scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs (npm run size-budget): zero-dep script sums the JS the built /app/ shell loads and fails over a 200 KiB ceiling (baseline ~166.6 KiB → ~33 KiB headroom); wired into CI right after the build. (3) De-dup'd via option (b): the Playwright webServer now serves the already-built dist/ in CI (process.env.CI ? serve-only : build+serve) so CI builds once (its own step, which also feeds the size budget) while local `npm run test:e2e` stays one command. (4) Added a concurrency group (cancel-in-progress on non-main refs) and an actions/cache for ~/.cache/ms-playwright keyed on the resolved @playwright/test version (install-deps-only on cache hit). Verified: build, size-budget, full npm test, and all 28 e2e (via the CI serve-only path) green.

items.title: Verify the 9 beta CSV adapters against real exports
items.prompt: Spun off from the R1 audit (2026-06-29). Only the TradingView adapter is verified; the other adapters in src/lib/adapters.ts (Tradovate, Rithmic, SierraChart, TradeStation, MotiveWave, Webull, IBKR, Schwab) are marked beta. Validate each against a REAL export from that platform — symbol/root, side, qty, PnL, date/time, and (for fills exports) the FIFO round-trip pairing + holdMs — and add a representative fixture to scripts/test-adapters.mjs for each verified format. Drop the beta flag per adapter only once validated, and note it in data/changelog.json. Needs real statements (external dependency); ship incrementally, one adapter per pass. Done when: at least one more adapter is verified end-to-end with a fixture and its beta flag cleared.

items.title: Create a feedback form
items.prompt: Add a way for users to send feedback. HARD constraint: this must never carry trade data or PII off-device beyond what the user explicitly types (the local-compute pillar) — no auto-attaching of their journal/trades. Evaluate the delivery path: a mailto: link (zero backend, simplest, A28-clean) vs a small Cloudflare Pages Function endpoint (functions/api/*, with spam/rate-limit protection like the existing admin endpoints). Keep it on a discoverable but non-intrusive control (e.g. the topbar Contact area). Respect CSP (no third-party form embeds). Done when: a feedback affordance ships (decide mailto vs endpoint), demo stays non-mutating, and tests/build are green.

items.title: Evaluate supporting TradingView’s other CSV export types
items.prompt: EVALUATION. TradingView can export several CSV shapes (e.g. the Strategy Tester / List-of-trades exports, account-manager history, and the balance/closed-position export the current adapter targets). Inventory which formats exist, which the existing TradingView adapter in src/lib/adapters.ts handles, and which are worth supporting. For each candidate: does it map cleanly to the normalized Trade shape (time/date/pnl/symbol/root/side/qty/holdMs), or does it need fills-pairing? Propose the best USER FLOW (auto-detect via sniff() vs an explicit picker in the landing/import UI). Keep compute()/costModel() unchanged (A29 — adapters normalize). Done when: a written evaluation + recommendation (which formats, detection strategy) is delivered, with adapter work spun off as its own item(s).

items.title: How-to: add per-platform CSV-export screenshots + clearer steps
items.prompt: Improve the How-To wiki (src/site/components/Howto.svelte) so a new user can reliably export the right CSV from each supported platform. Add per-platform screenshots of the export flow and tighten the step-by-step instructions (where the export lives, which option to pick, common gotchas). Images go through Vite's asset pipeline (src/assets/ or co-located, fingerprinted) and must respect the CSP/img-src; keep the page prerendered (SSG, A69) and the bundle lean (A28/size-budget). Done when: each supported platform has clear instructions + at least one screenshot, and the page builds + prerenders cleanly.

items.title: Create architecture diagrams for /docs
items.prompt: Add architecture diagrams to docs/ to make the system legible at a glance. Cover at least: the build/deploy pipeline (Vite multi-page -> dist/ -> Cloudflare Pages + /functions/*; the vite-ssg prerender), the runtime surfaces (app/demo/staging mounting one SPA, the Store seam), and the data flow (CSV -> adapters -> compute/costModel -> view). Prefer text-based, diffable diagrams (Mermaid in Markdown) so they version cleanly and need no binary assets (A28). Link them from docs/architecture.md. Done when: the diagrams are committed under docs/ and referenced from the architecture doc.

items.title: Discussion/doc: data flow & data-management overview
items.prompt: Write a concise overview (docs/) of how data moves through the app: CSV upload -> Adapters.detect/parse (normalization + fills pairing) -> Store.addTrades delta-merge -> IndexedDB persistence (the trades/journal/meta/trademeta object stores) -> loadRefData ref-data -> applyFilters -> compute/costModel -> view. Clarify what is transformed vs stored raw, the Store seam (real IndexedDB vs in-memory DemoStore), the stable tradeId/dedupe, and the local-compute/no-egress guarantee. Largely documents existing behavior (cross-check against store.ts/adapters.ts/core.ts). Done when: a written data-flow + data-management overview is committed under docs/ (pairs well with A109's diagrams).

items.title: Discussion: re-evaluate staging gating now that all surfaces share one SPA bundle
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Since the A33 cutover, app/demo/staging all mount ONE Svelte bundle and 'staging-only' features are gated at runtime by PAGE_MODE/isStaging (e.g. F23, A97) — meaning the code still ships to prod/demo, just hidden. Re-examine whether this is the right model for using staging as a genuine proving ground before prod/demo: options include the current runtime gating, a build-time flag/define to tree-shake staging-only code out of the prod bundle, an admin feature-flag (the existing /api/config + APP_FLAGS path), or a separate entry. Weigh against A28 (bundle size/size-budget), CH16 (promotion), the single-SPA simplicity (A33), and the no-divergent-code goal. Done when: a written recommendation on the staging-gating strategy (+ any spun-off items) is delivered.

items.title: Discussion: free SAST options for the CI pipeline
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Survey free static-analysis (SAST) tools we could add to CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) for this stack (TS + Svelte + Cloudflare Functions): e.g. GitHub CodeQL (free for the repo's visibility tier), Semgrep OSS, npm/osv dependency scanning, and any Svelte/TS-aware linters beyond the current ESLint. For each: setup cost, signal/noise, false-positive burden, runtime in CI, and fit with A28 (no heavy/unpinned tooling) and the existing gates. Recommend which (if any) to adopt and how to wire them (a separate workflow vs a CI step, fail vs report). Done when: a written shortlist with a recommendation is delivered (adoption spun off as its own item).

items.title: Evaluate gridstack.js vs a custom build for the resizable tiling grid (CSP/A28)
items.prompt: EVALUATION (spun off from R13; the chosen direction is a TILING dashboard grid, library-vs-custom undecided). Evaluate gridstack.js (and any equivalent framework-agnostic grid lib) for the resizable/snapping dashboard grid against THIS repo's constraints: (1) the `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55) — does the lib inject inline style="" attributes or a <style> element (CSP-VIOLATING), or apply geometry via the CSSOM (element.style.setProperty, which CSP allows)? This is the make-or-break test. (2) A28 dependency discipline — bundle size vs the size-budget (A96), pinned + audited + SRI-able, and the local-compute/no-egress gate (no telemetry). (3) fit with Svelte 5 runes + the existing Panel / Store.local / workspace-template system (does it own the DOM in a way that fights Svelte's reactivity or the A108 registry?). (4) mobile degradation + a11y (keyboard move/resize). Compare against a CUSTOM minimal grid built on our drag-reorder + styleProps + Store.local primitives. Done when: a written recommendation (gridstack vs custom, with the explicit CSP verdict) + the concrete phase-1 scope for F29 is delivered.

items.title: (staging) Resizable tiling dashboard grid — phase 1: column width + snap
items.prompt: (STAGING) Resizable tiling dashboard grid — PHASE 1 (spun off from R13; gated on the R20 library-vs-custom eval). First increment toward Quantower-style resizable/snapping modules, scoped tight: make the F26 parallel-grid columns (cal/cost/adv) RESIZABLE by WIDTH — drag a column edge, SNAP to a fixed track grid (e.g. 12-col), neighbors reflow — building on the existing primitives (Panel drag, the .modgrid in App.svelte, the styleProps CSSOM action for CSP-clean dimensions, the A108 module registry, and the Store.local seam for persistence). Persist per-module width (extend the staging-namespaced layout keys + workspace templates; migrate the old 1-D order). MUST: degrade to the current single-column stack on narrow/mobile; stay CSP-clean (no inline style="" — geometry via styleProps / CSS grid spans, A55); rAF-throttle the resize drag; keep compute() untouched (resize changes layout, not data); a11y keyboard fallback for resize. Staging-gated (STAGING_PAGE) until promoted (CH16). Later phases (height resize, full snap/reflow, drag-between-cells) are SEPARATE items. Done when: grid columns resize + snap + persist on staging, mobile degrades cleanly, build + npm test + e2e green.

items.title: Effective-dated (historical) broker commission rates
items.prompt: Commission rates change over time, so calculating commissions on OLDER trades with TODAY's rates is inaccurate. RESEARCH the history of commission rates for each broker in static/data/brokers.json (the per-broker micro/std per-side tiers) — when each broker's rates changed and the prior values — and restructure brokers.json to carry EFFECTIVE-DATED rate tiers (e.g. each broker holds a list of {effectiveFrom, comm:{micro,std}} entries, newest last), bump schemaVersion, and regenerate the manifest (scripts/build-manifest.mjs). Then update costModel() in src/lib/core.ts to pick the rate effective on EACH trade's date instead of a single current rate, keeping the existing API/shape so compute() is unaffected (A29). Backward-compatible: a broker with one tier behaves exactly as today. Add node fixtures (test-tax.mjs or a new suite) covering a trade before AND after a rate change. NOTE the research needs real sources (broker fee schedules) — cite them; ship incrementally per broker if needed. Done when: brokers.json carries dated rate history, costModel applies the date-correct rate per trade (with tests), build-manifest is regenerated, and npm test is green. Pairs with R21 (brokers.json/exchange-fees.json review).

items.title: Discussion: brokers.json <-> exchange-fees.json relationship
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Review the relationship between static/data/brokers.json (per-broker commission tiers: micro/std per-side) and static/data/exchange-fees.json (CME exchange/clearing/NFA fees + the micro set) and how costModel() in src/lib/core.ts combines them (broker commission + per-contract exchange fee -> per-symbol rate). Assess: overlap/duplication, whether the split is the right model, how the 'unknown exchange fee' fallback interacts with broker rates, schema consistency (schemaVersion, units), and how a future effective-dated rate history (F30) should be structured across BOTH files (do exchange/clearing/NFA fees also change over time?). Recommend keep/restructure + any spun-off items. Done when: a written assessment + recommendation is delivered. Adjacent: F30 (historical commission rates).

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
